title: Local-Global Attention Split
research_question: Does the current model really need full global attention in every layer, or can a local-global split preserve quality while reducing compute pressure?

candidates.name: l1-lower-local-256
candidates.goal: Replace lower-layer global attention with local-window attention while keeping upper layers global.
candidates.hypothesis: Early layers may not need global context yet, so local attention in the lower stack can save cost without hurting the final score much.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether local attention is safest in the early stack.
candidates.why_worthy: This targets a major structural assumption: global attention everywhere may be overkill in a small model.
candidates.description: use a 256-token local window in the lower four attention layers

candidates.name: l2-upper-local-256
candidates.goal: Flip the placement and test local attention in the upper stack instead.
candidates.hypothesis: Later layers may mostly refine local features, letting the upper stack use cheaper local attention while lower layers keep global routing.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether local attention is safer late than early.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly separates two opposing placement theories.
candidates.description: use a 256-token local window in the upper four attention layers

candidates.name: l3-middle-local-256
candidates.goal: Test whether the middle of the network is the easiest place to localize attention.
candidates.hypothesis: The middle stack may tolerate local attention best if lower layers build representations and upper layers handle global refinement.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether a middle local band is cleaner than top-only or bottom-only.
candidates.why_worthy: This gives a true third structural pattern, not just a mirror image of the first two.
candidates.description: use a 256-token local window in the middle four attention layers

candidates.name: l4-alternating-local-256
candidates.goal: Interleave local and global attention instead of grouping them by region.
candidates.hypothesis: Alternating local and global layers may let the model refresh global context without paying for it in every block.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests a distinct architecture theory rather than just another location choice.
candidates.description: alternate local-window and global attention layers through the stack

candidates.name: l5-lower-local-128
candidates.goal: Push the local-global idea harder after the safest lower-stack pattern.
candidates.hypothesis: If lower-stack local attention is the right placement, a smaller window may buy meaningful savings before quality collapses.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the local-global split has real room to trade locality for efficiency.
candidates.why_worthy: This tells us whether a promising local placement has headroom or immediately cliffs.
candidates.description: use a 128-token local window in the lower four attention layers
